verses.4: Let us choose for us that which is right.
verses.5: For Job has said, ‘I am righteous,
verses.6: Notwithstanding my right I am considered a liar.
verses.8: who goes in company with the workers of iniquity,
verses.9: For he has said, ‘It profits a man nothing
verses.10: “Therefore listen to me, you men of understanding:
verses.11: For the work of a man he will give to him,
verses.12: Yes surely, God will not do wickedly,
verses.13: Who put him in charge of the earth?
verses.14: If he set his heart on himself,
verses.16: “If now you have understanding, hear this.
verses.17: Should even one who hates justice govern?
verses.19: He doesn’t respect the persons of princes,
verses.20: In a moment they die, even at midnight.
verses.21: “For his eyes are on the ways of a man.
verses.22: There is no darkness, nor thick gloom,
verses.23: For he doesn’t need to consider a man further,
verses.24: He breaks mighty men in pieces in ways past finding out,
verses.25: Therefore he takes knowledge of their works.
verses.26: He strikes them as wicked men
verses.27: because they turned away from following him,
verses.28: so that they caused the cry of the poor to come to him.
verses.29: When he gives quietness, who then can condemn?
verses.30: that the godless man may not reign,
verses.32: Teach me that which I don’t see.
verses.33: Shall his recompense be as you desire, that you refuse it?
verses.34: Men of understanding will tell me,
verses.36: I wish that Job were tried to the end,
verses.37: For he adds rebellion to his sin.
